mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero (FEDFUNDS < 0.25%) signals monetary emergency accommodation. Under this regime, equity risk premiums compress and discount rates fall, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors rotate into equities seeking yield.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.18% as of May 2009 indicates emergency policy
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window in zero-rate regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month decline in unemployment rate (UNRATE falling by > 0.2 percentage points) signals labor market stabilization and recovery momentum. This reversal of the crisis deterioration triggers positive equity sentiment and forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings expectations improve.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 9.4% in May 2009, elevated but potentially stabilizing
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following UNRATE declines > 0.2pp are not significantly positive (mean return < 1%, t-test p > 0.10), or signal triggers false positives in > 50% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising month-over-month signals stabilization in residential construction demand and consumer confidence in asset values. This reversal of the housing collapse triggers positive equity returns over 5 trading days as cyclical sectors anticipate demand recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 458k units in April 2009, recovering from crisis lows
failure_condition: 5-day SPX returns following HOUST increases > 3% are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.3%, t-test p > 0.15), or the signal produces Rank IC < 0.02 over any 18-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month growth rate accelerates above its 3-month moving average, signaling strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side recovery. This mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns as the market reprices inflation risk and Fed tightening expectations upward.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 1-month growth rate exceeds its 3-month moving average by > 0.5pp
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 97.07 in April 2009, recovering from crisis trough
regime_hint: early-cycle supply recovery with inflation concerns
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following INDPRO acceleration signals are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) both rise month-over-month simultaneously, indicating coordinated consumer and residential demand recovery. This joint signal of broad-based demand reacceleration predicts positive 20-day SPX returns as earnings revisions accelerate across consumer-sensitive sectors.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS rises > 1% AND HOUST rises > 2% month-over-month in same month
evidence_refs: fact:Both series recovering from 2008-2009 crisis lows
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns following joint RSAFS and HOUST increases are not significantly higher than returns following single-series increases (t-test p > 0.10), or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) month-over-month growth rate turns positive after 12+ consecutive months of decline, signaling the inflection point from job destruction to job creation. This regime shift predicts positive 60-day SPX returns as the market reprices recession probability downward and earnings growth expectations improve.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS month-over-month growth rate > 0% after 12+ months of negative growth
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 132,151k in May 2009, still below pre-crisis peak but stabilizing
regime_hint: labor market inflection from contraction to expansion
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns following PAYEMS inflection are not significantly positive (mean return < 2%, t-test p > 0.10), or the signal produces false positives in > 45% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) year-over-year growth rate falls below 2% while FEDFUNDS remains near zero, signaling deflationary pressure and policy accommodation persistence. Under this regime, real discount rates remain deeply negative, supporting equity valuations and predicting positive 20-day returns.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 212.67 in April 2009, low inflation environment
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns under joint low-inflation and low-rates regime are not significantly positive (mean return < 1%, t-test p > 0.12), or interaction effect Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) quarter-over-quarter annualized rate turns positive after two consecutive quarters of contraction, signaling recession end. This inflection point predicts negative 5-day SPX returns as the market reprices the magnitude of the recovery surprise downward and volatility spikes on profit-taking.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth rate > 0% after 2+ quarters of negative growth
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 11,353.7B in Q1 2009, likely still in contraction but near inflection
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns following GDPC1 inflection are positive in > 60% of instances, or the signal produces Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month window.
